hero.badge: Saigon, Cu Chi & Mui Ne Beach — 5 Days
hero.title: 5 Days — Saigon, Cu Chi Tunnels & Mui Ne Beach.
hero.titleAccent: White Dunes at Dawn, Underground History & City Lights.
hero.intro: Five days of South Vietnam's widest contrast: the Mui Ne sand dune coast, Saigon's war history streets, and the Cu Chi Tunnels 40 kilometres underground from Ho Chi Minh City. Two nights in Mui Ne — White Sand Dunes at dawn (04:45 departure), ATV through the dune field, Fairy Stream's red clay canyon, sunset on the Red Sand Dunes. Then Saigon: War Remnants Museum, Independence Palace, and the Nguyen Hue street scene. Day four: Cu Chi — 250 kilometres of wartime tunnel network, now accessible to civilians.

overview.heading: 5 Days Saigon + Cu Chi Tunnels + Mui Ne Beach Resort
body: This five-day package covers South Vietnam's most distinctive landscapes and experiences: the Mui Ne beach and dune coast, the Cu Chi underground wartime tunnel network, and Ho Chi Minh City's essential historical sites. Two nights in Mui Ne gives the full coastal experience — the White Sand Dunes at dawn, the Fairy Stream, the fishing village — without the early morning rush that single-night packages require.

The Cu Chi Tunnels are among the most powerful sites in Vietnam for historical understanding. The Ben Dinh section of the tunnel complex, 40 kilometres northwest of Ho Chi Minh City, was the northern section of the Viet Cong's wartime tunnel network — 250 kilometres of tunnels at up to three levels below ground, used for communication, supply, field hospitals, and surprise attacks during the American War. A local guide leads visitors through the tunnel sections (widened for tourist access), the hidden trapdoors, booby trap displays, and the tank and bomb craters left from B-52 strikes.

Saigon on day three covers the major historical sites: the War Remnants Museum (the most visited museum in Vietnam), Independence Palace, Notre Dame Cathedral, and the General Post Office. Day three evening on Nguyen Hue Street and the Café Apartment Block at 42 Nguyen Hue gives the modern Ho Chi Minh City street scene.

highlights.heading: Why This 5-Day Saigon Cu Chi Mui Ne Package Stands Apart

items.title: White Sand Dunes at Dawn — 04:45 Departure
items.body: The White Sand Dunes (Bau Trang, 25 km north of Mui Ne) are best experienced at sunrise — cool sand underfoot, shifting colour from lavender pre-dawn through gold and orange as the sun rises. The 04:45 jeep pickup reaches the dunes by 05:15–05:30, giving a full hour of optimal light before the sand heats up. The ATV ride (2 persons per vehicle) covers the full dune field. The small freshwater lakes that sit next to the white dunes — surrounded by water hyacinth — are visible as reflective silvers in the early light.
items.imageAlt: Mui Ne White Sand Dunes sunrise ATV Vietnam

items.title: Cu Chi Tunnels — 250km of Underground Viet Cong Network
items.body: Cu Chi is 40 km northwest of Ho Chi Minh City — a 90-minute drive from the city centre. The Ben Dinh section of the tunnel network is the most visitable: the guide shows the hidden entrances (trapdoors concealed under leaf litter), the tunnel sections that have been widened to 80 cm for visitors (the originals were 50×80 cm — Vietnamese soldiers only), the underground kitchens with smoke-venting systems designed to dissipate smoke through tree roots, and the field hospital. Outside: the bomb craters from B-52 strikes, the anti-tank traps, and a demonstration of crossbow booby traps.
items.imageAlt: Cu Chi Tunnels underground wartime network Vietnam Saigon

items.title: Fairy Stream — Red Clay Canyon Walk
items.body: The Fairy Stream (Suoi Tien) is a narrow canyon of deep red and yellow laterite clay, 3 km from Mui Ne Beach, with a shallow clear stream running through it. The walk is ankle-deep through the stream — no shoes — past canyon walls of sculpted red clay that get taller as you go in. The overhead jungle canopy keeps the canyon shaded and cool. The colour of the canyon walls against the bright clear water and the vivid green tropical plants above is completely unique in Vietnam. A 30-minute walk in and back.
items.imageAlt: Fairy Stream red clay canyon Mui Ne Vietnam

items.title: Saigon — War Museum & Café Apartment Block
items.body: Ho Chi Minh City's essential sites concentrate South Vietnam's 20th-century history: the War Remnants Museum (photographs and artefacts documenting the American War from the Vietnamese perspective — the most visited museum in the country), Independence Palace (the Reunification Palace, preserved as it was on 30 April 1975), Notre Dame Cathedral, and the colonial-era General Post Office. The evening Nguyen Hue pedestrian boulevard and the Café Apartment Block (42 Nguyen Hue, a six-storey residential building where every apartment is a different café) give the modern HCMC experience.
items.imageAlt: Saigon Ho Chi Minh City War Remnants Museum Notre Dame Vietnam

itinerary.heading: Your 5-Day Saigon, Cu Chi & Mui Ne Itinerary

days.title: Arrive Saigon — Transfer to Mui Ne
days.description: Arrive at Tan Son Nhat International Airport (Ho Chi Minh City). Guide meets guests at arrival hall. Transfer to Mui Ne (225 km, approximately 3.5 hours). Guide introduction at 18:30 at Mui Ne hotel. Dinner at local beach restaurant. Early rest recommended: 04:45 departure for White Sand Dunes tomorrow morning.

days.title: White Sand Dunes Sunrise, ATV, Red Dunes Sunset
days.description: 04:45: Jeep pickup for White Sand Dunes (25 km north). Sunrise at Bau Trang white quartz dune field. ATV ride through dunes (2 persons per vehicle). 07:30: Return to hotel for breakfast. 09:00: Fairy Stream walk (red clay canyon, ankle-deep clear stream, 30 min each way). Traditional fishing village (bamboo coracle boats, junk fleet, morning wet market). Lunch. Phan Thiet town: 225-year-old market, Duc Thanh School (where Ho Chi Minh taught 1910–1911), Pohsanu Cham Towers (9th–15th century Hindu ruins, southernmost Cham site in Vietnam). 17:30: Red Sand Dunes sunset (2 km from resort beach — the orange dunes glow deep red at dusk). Seafood dinner.

days.title: Transfer to Saigon — City Tour
days.description: Checkout and transfer to Ho Chi Minh City (225 km, 3.5 hours). Arrive for Saigon city tour: War Remnants Museum, Independence Palace (Reunification Palace), Notre Dame Cathedral, General Post Office. Lunch at local restaurant. Hotel check-in. Afternoon: Nguyen Hue pedestrian boulevard, 42 Nguyen Hue Café Apartment Block, Ben Thanh Market.
suggestedActivities: Checkout and transfer to Saigon (3.5 hrs)

days.title: Cu Chi Tunnels
days.description: Morning drive to Cu Chi Tunnels (40 km, approximately 90 minutes). Guided tour of Ben Dinh tunnel complex with local guide: hidden tunnel entrance trapdoors, widened tunnel sections (visitors can crawl), underground kitchen with smoke-dissipating chimney system, field hospital, booby trap displays, B-52 bomb craters, anti-tank barriers. Visitors may fire AK-47 or M-16 at the shooting range (own expense). Lunch in Cu Chi. Return to Saigon (90 minutes). Afternoon shopping: Saigon Square, Vincom Centre. Dinner at own arrangement.
suggestedActivities: Drive to Cu Chi Tunnels (90 min)
Ben Dinh tunnel complex guided tour
Booby trap and B-52 crater displays
Shopping at Saigon Square or Vincom

days.title: Saigon Free Morning — Depart
days.description: Free morning in Ho Chi Minh City. Options: Cho Lon Chinatown (Binh Tay Market, Thien Hau Pagoda), the Fine Arts Museum, or a final Nguyen Hue coffee. Transfer to Tan Son Nhat International Airport per agreed schedule.

whyBook.heading: South Vietnam's Complete Experience — By Malaysia's Trusted Agency Since 1988
whyBook.body: Saigon, Mui Ne, and Cu Chi cover three completely different South Vietnam experiences — beach coast, wartime history underground, and the modern city. Dhesu has been arranging South Vietnam circuits for Malaysian travellers since 1988. Our guides time the White Sand Dunes sunrise precisely, lead the Cu Chi tunnel tour with genuine local knowledge, and handle every transfer between the three very different locations.

points.title: MATTA & IATA Registered
points.body: Fully licensed Malaysian travel agency. South Vietnam circuits are among our most booked itineraries for Malaysian travellers.

points.title: 35+ Years of Vietnam Experience
points.body: Since 1988, we have been running South Vietnam tours for Malaysians. Our Mui Ne and Saigon guides are trusted long-term partners with years of local expertise.

points.title: Cu Chi Tunnels + White Dunes Sunrise Included
points.body: The Cu Chi Tunnels guided tour with entry fee, the 04:45 White Sand Dunes jeep transfer, and the ATV ride are all included. No separate booking needed at either site.

points.title: Buy 3 Free 1 Promotion
points.body: Groups of 4 travelling together pay for 3 — the 4th person travels free. Ask about this offer when booking.

sections.heading: 5 Day Saigon Cu Chi Tunnels Mui Ne Beach Tour from Malaysia
sections.body:
The 5-day Saigon, Cu Chi Tunnels, and Mui Ne package is the most complete South Vietnam itinerary for Malaysian travellers who want both historical depth and beach time. Two nights in Mui Ne gives the full sand dune experience without early morning pressure: the White Sand Dunes at dawn, the Fairy Stream, the fishing village, and the Red Dunes at sunset — all in a single memorable Mui Ne day.

Cu Chi Tunnels, 40 km from Saigon, is the most visited historical site in South Vietnam after the War Remnants Museum. The 250-km underground tunnel network used by the Viet Cong during the American War is now accessible to visitors — a powerful and informative half-day experience that explains the wartime landscape through which the rest of the Saigon city tour is filtered.

faq.heading: Questions About the 5-Day Saigon Cu Chi Mui Ne Package

items.question: Do Malaysians need a visa for Vietnam?
items.answer: No. Malaysian passport holders enter Vietnam visa-free for up to 30 days. Arrive at Tan Son Nhat International Airport with your Malaysian passport.

items.question: Is it necessary to crawl through the Cu Chi Tunnels?
items.answer: No. The tunnel crawl section is optional — visitors who prefer not to crawl can observe the tunnel entrance and follow the guide through the above-ground exhibits (booby traps, kitchen ventilation system, bomb craters) without entering the tunnels. For those who do enter, the tunnels have been widened from the original 50×80 cm to approximately 80×120 cm, and are lit. Most visitors find it memorable rather than uncomfortable.

items.question: How does this compare to the 4-day Saigon Mui Ne package?
items.answer: The 4-day package (pkgid 2107) does not include Cu Chi Tunnels and covers only 1 night in Saigon. This 5-day package adds Cu Chi Tunnels (a full half-day including the drive) and 2 nights in Saigon. If Cu Chi is a priority or you want more Saigon time, the 5-day version is the better choice.

items.question: Why is the White Sand Dunes departure at 04:45 AM?
items.answer: The dunes are most beautiful and most comfortable at sunrise. The sand heats quickly once the sun is up, and the photographic light is best in the pre-dawn to mid-morning window. The 04:45 jeep pickup reaches the dunes around 05:15–05:30, giving a full hour before the heat sets in. The ATV ride and lake views are also significantly more pleasant in the morning cool.
